content.full_text:
July 9, 1975

Senator Ruben S. Ayala  
State Capitol, Room 4068  
Sacramento, Ca 95814

Dear Senator Ayala:

This letter is in response to your inquiry regarding an offer by the United Auto Workers to pay air fare for you and your wife so that you may participate in the National Congress of Hispanic American Citizens to be held in Washington, D.C. during the week of July 16th.

This proposed payment raises the following issues under the Political Reform Act:

1. Under Government Code Section 86204, it is unlawful for you to receive a gift when a lobbyist has made, has acted as an agent or intermediary in the making of, or has arranged for the making of the gift. (This prohibition does not apply to gifts aggregating less than ten dollars in value in any one month.) The United Auto Workers employs a legislative advocate in Sacramento. If that person has taken any part in offering the air tickets or arranging for the tickets to be given, you are prohibited from accepting them.

2. If no lobbyist participates in the proffered gift, there is no restriction in the Political Reform Act which would prohibit you from accepting that gift. However, you must report the gift on your annual Statement of Economic Interests. As required by Government Code Section 87207, you would have to disclose the name and address of the source of the gift, a general description of the business activity of the source, the value and the date on which the gift was received.

This letter is not a formal opinion of the Commission, issued under Government Code Section 83114, but reflects the informal views of the staff.

I hope this will be of assistance to you.

Sincerely,

Natalie E. West, Counsel
